req.title: Approve leave request — variant 5
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) a manager approving an assigned leave request with 2FA confirmation yielding state=approved, employee notification, and audit entry (positive/EP); (2) a non-assignee manager attempting approval returning 403 (negative/NEG); (3) approving a request on the 5th business day boundary — the last valid day before auto-escalation (boundary/BVA); (4) attempting to approve an already-actioned request returning 409 — this is a state-transition conflict test, correctly classified as negative/NEG since it exercises an invalid state transition error pathway; (5) a decision-table case where 2FA is skipped causing rejection (negative/DT). Together these provide EP, BVA, NEG, and DT coverage aligned with ISO/IEC/IEEE 29119-4. Test[3] was previously miscategorised as negative/STC; STC applies to positive state-transition sequences, not error pathways. The 409 conflict scenario is an error pathway (negative/NEG) per the requirement's error_pathways field.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_request_with_2fa
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending and is assigned to this manager
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with valid manager session and 2FA token
expected: Audit log entry created for the action
trace: R-WEB-066: request state transitions to approved
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_manager_approves_assigned_request_with_2fa(
    manager_session,        # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    pending_leave_request,  # <fixture>: returns {"id": <id>, "assignee_id": manager_id}
    valid_2fa_token,        # <fixture>: returns current TOTP token for manager
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: client to query audit service
    notification_sink,      # <fixture>: captures outbound notifications
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    payload = {"otp": valid_2fa_token}

    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve",
        json=payload,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["state"] == "approved"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(resource_id=req_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in audit_entries)

    notifications = notification_sink.received_for(pending_leave_request["employee_id"])
    assert any(n["type"] == "leave_approved" for n in notifications)

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403
preconditions: Leave request exists with state=pending and is assigned to a DIFFERENT manager
Valid 2FA token available for the acting manager
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with non-assignee manager session and valid 2FA token
expected: Response body contains error code indicating forbidden/not-assignee
No audit entry created for approval
trace: R-WEB-066: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_assignee_manager_approve_returns_403(
    other_manager_session,          # <fixture>: session for manager B
    pending_leave_request,          # <fixture>: request assigned to manager A
    valid_2fa_token_other_manager,  # <fixture>: TOTP token for manager B
    api_client,                     # <fixture>: unauthenticated read client
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    payload = {"otp": valid_2fa_token_other_manager}

    resp = other_manager_session.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve",
        json=payload,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "not_assignee" in body.get("error_code", "") or \
           "forbidden" in body.get("message", "").lower()

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "pending"

tests.name: test_approve_on_5th_business_day_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and is the assignee
Leave request was created exactly 5 business days ago (boundary: last valid action day)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve on the 5th business day with valid manager session and 2FA token
expected: Request is NOT escalated to hr-admin
trace: R-WEB-066: within 5 business days — boundary upper limit of valid action window
R-WEB-066: unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin (boundary: action on day 5 prevents escalation)
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_approve_on_5th_business_day_boundary(
    manager_session,              # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    leave_request_aged_5_bdays,   # <fixture>: request created exactly 5 business days ago
    valid_2fa_token,              # <fixture>: TOTP token
    audit_log_client,             # <fixture>
    escalation_log_client,        # <fixture>: queries hr-admin escalation records
):
    req_id = leave_request_aged_5_bdays["id"]
    payload = {"otp": valid_2fa_token}

    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve",
        json=payload,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    escalations = escalation_log_client.get_escalations(resource_id=req_id)
    assert not escalations, "Request must not be escalated when approved on day 5"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(resource_id=req_id)
    assert any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in audit_entries)

tests.name: test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and is the assignee
Leave request already has state=approved (previously actioned)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve on a request already in approved state
expected: Response body contains conflict error indicating request already actioned
trace: R-WEB-066: error pathway — 409 if already actioned
R-WEB-066: re-approving an already-approved request is an invalid operation and must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_already_actioned_request_returns_409(
    manager_session,               # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    approved_leave_request,        # <fixture>: request already in state=approved
    valid_2fa_token,               # <fixture>: TOTP token
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request["id"]
    payload = {"otp": valid_2fa_token}

    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve",
        json=payload,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert "already_actioned" in body.get("error_code", "") or \
           "conflict" in body.get("message", "").lower()

tests.name: test_approve_without_2fa_token_is_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated user has role=manager and is the assignee
No 2FA token is provided in the request body (2FA=absent)
actions: POST /leave-requests/{id}/approve with valid manager session but WITHOUT otp field
expected: Response body indicates 2FA confirmation is required
No audit entry for approval created
trace: R-WEB-066: DT row — manager=valid, assignee=yes, 2FA=absent => request rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_approve_without_2fa_token_is_rejected(
    manager_session,        # <fixture>: authenticated manager HTTP session
    pending_leave_request,  # <fixture>: request assigned to this manager
    audit_log_client,       # <fixture>: client to query audit service
    api_client,             # <fixture>: read client
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    payload = {}  # deliberately omit otp

    resp = manager_session.post(
        f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve",
        json=payload,
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (401, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "2fa" in body.get("message", "").lower() or \
           "otp" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
           "two_factor" in body.get("error_code", "").lower()

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "pending"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(resource_id=req_id)
    assert not any(e["action"] == "approve" for e in audit_entries)
